senses.definition: A strong rope fixed along a high place such as a ship or between supports, used as a lifeline for people to hold on to for safety.
examples: The sailor clipped his harness onto the ridgerope before stepping onto the narrow ledge.
During the storm, the crew held the ridgerope as the ship rolled from side to side.

senses.definition: Farm machines or tools used to make raised lines of earth called ridges, especially for planting crops like potatoes.
examples: Modern ridgers can form neat, even rows across a whole field in a single pass.
The farmer adjusted the ridgers to make higher ridges for the wet soil.

senses.definition: Long narrow raised lines on a surface, such as on land, skin, or another material.
examples: Snow lay deep in the valleys between the ridges.
The ridges on the coin had been worn smooth with use.

senses.definition: The highest, narrow line at the top of a long hill or mountain.
examples: From the ridgetop we could see the town spread out below.
They set up their tents along the ridgetop to catch the cool breeze.

senses.definition: Several highest, narrow lines at the tops of long hills or mountains.
examples: Clouds drifted over the ridgetops in the late afternoon.
The park protects forests on the ridgetops and in the valleys.

senses.definition: A tree standing along the top of a ridge, often used as a landmark.
examples: They aimed for the old ridgetree that marked the halfway point of the trail.
A lone ridgetree stood dark against the evening sky.

senses.definition: Trees that grow along the top of a ridge, often described together as a line or group.
examples: The storm broke branches from the ridgetrees along the hilltop.
At sunset the ridgetrees were black shapes against a red sky.

senses.definition: A path or road that runs along the top of a long hill or ridge.
examples: They followed the ancient ridgeway across the downs.
The ridgeway offers wide views over the countryside.

senses.definition: Several paths or roads that run along the tops of long hills or ridges.
examples: Old ridgeways link the hilltop villages in this region.
Over time, the ridgeways became important trade routes.

senses.definition: In the direction of, or along, a ridge or raised line.
examples: The fence runs ridgewise along the hill.
The planks were laid ridgewise to strengthen the frame.

senses.definition: Having more raised lines or ridges than something else.
examples: Her hands grew ridgier as she aged.
The ridgier surface gives better grip.

senses.definition: Having the most raised lines or ridges compared with others.
examples: This is the ridgiest section of the mountain range.
Choose the ridgiest mat if you want extra grip.

senses.definition: A young male horse with one or both testicles not properly descended.
examples: The vet advised castrating the ridgil for health and safety reasons.
Because he was a ridgil, the colt needed a careful medical check.

senses.definition: Young male horses with one or both testicles not properly descended.
examples: The farm kept the ridgils separate from the main herd.
Experienced breeders can often spot ridgils early.

senses.definition: The process of making raised lines of earth called ridges in a field.
examples: Ridging improves drainage on heavy soils.
Spring ridging helps prepare the land for planting potatoes.

senses.definition: The appearance of raised lines forming on a surface, especially skin, nails, or fabric.
examples: Nail ridging can be a sign of aging.
The leather shows some ridging after years of use.

senses.definition: In a way that forms or shows raised lines or ridges.
examples: The fabric stretched ridgingly across the frame.
The ground rose ridgingly toward the distant hills.

senses.definition: Raised lines or areas that look like ridges on a surface.
examples: Fine ridgings marked the surface of the old painting.
The ice had tiny ridgings formed by the wind.

senses.definition: A male horse with one or both testicles not properly descended.
examples: The ridgling was more aggressive than the other colts.
Surgery may be needed if a ridgling is used for riding.

senses.definition: Male horses with one or both testicles not properly descended.
examples: The ridglings were kept in a separate paddock.
Doctors recommend checking young colts for ridglings in the herd.

senses.definition: Having many long, narrow raised lines or areas like ridges.
examples: The ridgy coastline made travel difficult.
She massaged his tired, ridgy shoulders.

senses.definition: Easily brought to laughter and often very cheerful.
examples: His ridibund nature made him popular at parties.
The children were ridibund after hearing the funny story.

senses.definition: Unkind laughter or remarks that make someone or something seem silly or stupid.
examples: She was afraid of public ridicule if her plan failed.
His accent was often the subject of ridicule at school.

senses.definition: To laugh at someone or something in a way that is unkind and makes them seem silly.
examples: The press ridiculed the politician for his mistake.
She felt hurt when her friends ridiculed her ideas.

senses.definition: Was laughed at in an unkind way that made the person or thing seem silly.
examples: He was ridiculed by colleagues for suggesting such a simple solution.
The film was ridiculed by critics but loved by audiences.

senses.definition: A person who often makes fun of others in an unkind way.
examples: Nobody liked being around such a constant ridiculer.
The ridiculer eventually apologized for his cruel jokes.

senses.definition: People who often make fun of others in an unkind way.
examples: The ridiculers eventually lost their influence.
Online ridiculers can do real emotional harm.

senses.definition: Laughs at someone or something in an unkind way that makes them seem silly.
examples: The article ridicules the new policy as unrealistic.
He often ridicules other people's efforts instead of helping.

senses.definition: Different examples or acts of unkind laughter and remarks that make someone seem silly.
examples: His strange clothes attracted many ridicules from classmates.
The play has been the source of ridicules and jokes for years.

senses.definition: Laughing at someone or something in an unkind way that makes them seem silly.
examples: They were ridiculing his accent in front of everyone.
Instead of ridiculing her, they should offer help.

senses.definition: To treat someone or something as ridiculous, often by making them seem silly or absurd.
examples: Some commentators ridiculize any attempt at serious debate.
The cartoon was intended to ridiculize the government's new law.

senses.definition: The quality of being extremely silly or absurd.
examples: The sheer ridiculosity of the situation made everyone laugh.
Critics pointed out the ridiculosity of the proposal.

senses.definition: Very silly or unreasonable and deserving to be laughed at.
examples: That price is absolutely ridiculous.
He felt ridiculous in the bright pink suit.

senses.definition: In a way that is very silly or unreasonable.
examples: These shoes are ridiculously expensive.
He was ridiculously proud of his tiny garden.

senses.definition: The quality of being very silly or unreasonable.
examples: They laughed at the ridiculousness of the whole plan.
Her stories often highlight the ridiculousness of modern life.

senses.definition: Different examples or kinds of things that are very silly or unreasonable.
examples: The book lists the many ridiculousnesses of bureaucracy.
We laughed over the small ridiculousnesses of daily life.

senses.definition: Most full of laughter or amusement, compared with others.
examples: That was the ridiest evening they had spent together.
The comedy show promised the ridiest night of the year.

senses.definition: The activity or sport of sitting on a horse, bicycle, or motorbike and controlling it as it moves.
examples: She loves horse riding in the countryside.
We went riding along the river path.

senses.definition: A political or administrative area, especially a voting district in Canada.
examples: He was elected to represent the rural riding.
The party lost several key ridings in the last election.

senses.definition: Used for or while riding a horse, bicycle, or motorbike.
examples: She bought new riding boots.
A riding helmet is essential for safety.

senses.definition: A man whose job is to ride a horse, especially in older or historical contexts.
examples: The lord sent his ridingman to deliver the message.
A skilled ridingman could travel long distances in a day.

senses.definition: Men whose job is to ride horses, especially in older or historical contexts.
examples: The castle's ridingmen patrolled the borders.
His ridingmen were ready to leave at dawn.

senses.definition: Electoral or administrative districts, especially in Canada or formerly in parts of England.
examples: The party gained two suburban ridings.
Historically, Yorkshire was divided into three ridings.

senses.definition: A type of sea turtle, especially the Kemp's ridley or olive ridley, which are small and live in warm oceans.
examples: Conservationists work to protect the nesting sites of the olive ridley.
The Kemp's ridley is one of the most endangered sea turtles.

senses.definition: A family name or given name in English-speaking countries.
examples: Mr. Ridley will give a talk on climate change.
Ridley has published several science fiction novels.

senses.definition: Sea turtles of the ridley type, especially Kemp's ridley and olive ridley turtles.
examples: Ridleys come ashore in large groups to lay their eggs.
Pollution in the ocean threatens ridleys worldwide.

senses.definition: A grand public party or ball with music and often masks, popular in the 18th century.
examples: They attended a fashionable ridotto in Venice.
The novel describes a masked ridotto crowded with nobles.

senses.definition: Grand public parties or balls with music and often masks, popular in the 18th century.
examples: The city was famous for its lavish ridottos.
Letters from the time describe ridottos that lasted all night.

senses.definition: Removes or gets someone free from something unwanted.
examples: This update rids the software of several bugs.
She finally rids herself of the heavy old furniture.

senses.definition: A female given name, especially in Japanese and some European languages.
examples: Rie introduced herself to the class.
The novel follows Rie as she moves to the city.

senses.definition: A dark blue or black mineral in the amphibole group, sometimes found in a fibrous form known as blue asbestos.
examples: Riebeckite often occurs in certain granites and iron-rich rocks.
The fibrous variety of riebeckite is called crocidolite or blue asbestos.

senses.definition: Samples or pieces of the mineral riebeckite.
examples: The collection includes several riebeckites from different locations.
Under the microscope, the riebeckites show a strong blue colour.

senses.definition: The basic unit of money used in Cambodia.
examples: You can pay in US dollars or in riel in most shops in Cambodia.
The price of a bus ticket is usually given in riel.

senses.definition: Amounts of money in the Cambodian currency.
examples: He changed his dollars into riels at the border.
The meal cost about twenty thousand riels.

senses.definition: In South African English, a long leather strap or thong, especially used with animals.
examples: He cracked the riem to drive the cattle forward.
The old riem hung neatly on the stable wall.

senses.definition: Relating to the mathematical ideas of Riemann, especially Riemannian geometry; an alternative spelling of Riemannian.
examples: The paper discusses properties of riemannean manifolds.
Riemannean metrics generalize the notion of distance on curved spaces.
